ocr.text: The study by Ekangaki and Chung [5] in 2005 involved a sample size of 450 cancer patients under chemotherapy in two various
age-gender groups, utilizing a case-control design, and concluded that the application of histamines, phenothiazines,
butyrophenones, and anticholinergics to cope with CINV in chemotherapy brings about side effects. Sirin and Flynn [6] in 2013
examined 52 cancer patients under chemotherapy in two various age-gender groups and found that CINV was reduced by about
13.8% in the group receiving dimenhydrinate as compared to placebo. Pierce and Lin [7] in 2013 studied 85 over 30-year-old
men with prostate cancer under chemotherapy, discovering that prescribing dimenhydrinate reduced their CINV compared to
the control group. Sheanakul et al. [8] in 2011 analyzed 30 below 60-year-old cancer patients under chemotherapy in two
gender groups and determined that the continuous injection of midazolam reduced pain and CINV. Apfel et al. [9] in 2012
focused on 23 cancer patients aged 15–50 under chemotherapy and found that cinnarizine controls and reduces CINV.
Study  Year  Sample size  Design/population  Method  Outcome

Ekangaki and Chung [5]  2005  450  Cancer patients under chemotherapy in two various age-gender groups  Case-control  The application of histamines, phenothiazines, butyrophenones, and anticholinergics to cope with CINV in chemotherapy brings about side effects

Sirin and Flynn [6]  2013  52  Cancer patients under chemotherapy in two various age-gender groups  Case-control  CINV was reduced by about %13.8 in the group receiving dimenhydrinate as compared to placebo

Pierce and Lin [7]  2013  85  Over 30-year-old men with prostate cancer under chemotherapy  Case-control  Prescribing dimenhydrinate for men with prostate cancer under chemotherapy has reduced their CINV as compared to control group

Sheanakul et al. [8]  2011  30  Below 60-year-old cancer patients under chemotherapy in two gender groups  Case-control  The continuous injection of midazolam has reduced pain and CINV in test group as compared to control group

Apfel et al. [9]  2012  23  15–50 years of age cancer patients under chemotherapy in two gender groups  Case-control  Cinnarizine controls and reduces CINV in cancer patients under chemotherapy as compared to placebo group through hindering histaminic H1 receptor

Rosenthal et al. [10]  2010  64  Over 12-year-old cancer patients under chemotherapy in two various gender groups  Clinical trial  Metoclopramide reduces CINV in cancer patients under chemotherapy through enhancing the activity threshold of CTZ chemical receptors

Denehy et al. [11]  2011  85  Cancer patients under chemotherapy in two various age-gender groups  Case-control  CINV is less in cancer patients under chemotherapy receiving metoclopramide as compared to the group receiving placebo
Sample  n  Fat %  PBDE (ng/g ww)  PBDE (ng/g lw)
European hare  1  3.3  0.06  103
Rosenthal et al. [10] in 2010 studied 64 over 12-year-old cancer patients under chemotherapy, reporting that metoclopramide
reduces CINV. Denehy et al. [11] in 2011 examined 85 cancer patients under chemotherapy, concluding that CINV is less in
patients receiving metoclopramide compared to placebo. In Table 2, Baltic herring had a sample size of 12, with fat % at 9.5
(3.2) 6.0–19.0, PBDE (ng/g ww) at 0.4 (0.7) 0.0–2.0, and PBDE (ng/g lw) at 12.2 (7.0) 4.2–26.3. The grey seal's sample size
The mallard had a sample size of 1 (sub-n = 2) with fat % at 19.5, PBDE (ng/g ww) at 0.17, and PBDE (ng/g lw) at 0.88.
Reindeer had a sample size of 14, with fat % at 3.4 (1.34) 1.4–5.8, PBDE (ng/g ww) at 0.06 (0.11) 0.004–0.44, and PBDE (ng/g
lw) at 1.85 (2.52) 0.12–7.57. Moose had a sample size of 3, with fat % at 2 (0.96) 1.0–3.3, PBDE (ng/g ww) at 0.017 (0.006)
0.01–0.03, and PBDE (ng/g lw) at 1.24 (0.94) 0.36–2.55. The European hare had a sample size of 1, with fat % at 3.3, PBDE
(ng/g ww) at 0.06, and PBDE (ng/g lw) at 103.
